summary: Session 1 of 3 -- Policy surface + blast-radius predicate + artifact contract. Shipped the tier-orthogonal Path-Aware Critique policy foundations (no close-out gate yet; that is Session 2). (1) ATTRIBUTE: new ai_router/path_aware_critique.py adds the `pathAwareCritique: none|advisory|required` per-set policy attribute with the seed -> record-once-at-set-start -> immutable machinery, deliberately mirroring the Set-057 verificationMode pattern (own activity-log `kind: path_aware_critique`, default `none` = strictly opt-in, no gate). The spec field parses for free via the shared _extract_session_set_configuration_block (read_spec_path_aware_critique), exactly as verificationMode is read -- so no session_state.py/spec_config.py edit was needed. start_session gained --path-aware-critique + best-effort _capture_path_aware_critique wired after _capture_verification_mode (end-to-end smoke confirmed seed->record->read). (2) PREDICATE: new ai_router/blast_radius.py implements the proposal's core predicate P_set = any(P_task) -- classifies a set's changed/planned surface into cross-artifact/shared-schema/wiring/index and RECOMMENDS a value (required when P_set, advisory for low-blast code, none for docs/empty). Advisory only: operator confirms, never a hard auto-set. ASCII-only CLI (`python -m ai_router.blast_radius <paths>` + --json). (3) ARTIFACT: docs/path-aware-critique.schema.json (structural contract) + docs/path-aware-critique-schema.md + example, plus a pure-Python validate_path_aware_critique_artifact (no jsonschema, which is test-only) the Session-2 gate will call -- enforces >=2 DISTINCT providers (the 010-vs-C3 multi-provider lesson) and content-non-triviality. (4) DOCS: spec-md-schema.md + session-set-authoring-guide.md document the new field (tier-orthogonal; net-new Full-tier wiring deferred to S2). (5) TESTS: 70 new (attribute parse/seed/record/immutability; predicate classification + recommendation + CLI; artifact accept/reject; JSON-schema static contract + dual-validation drift guard). Full ai_router suite green: 1372 passed / 1 skipped. Cross-provider session verification (gpt-5.4): Round 1 ISSUES_FOUND with 3 Major -- one REAL (the pure-Python validator was more lenient than the JSON Schema on 4 structural cases: extra top-level key, non-array findings, non-string summary, finding missing description) which was FIXED (validator now matches the schema; accept-sets agree except the intended distinct-provider rule; pinned by new TestStructuralParity) -- and two FALSE POSITIVES disproven with evidence (start_session.py IS in _WIRING_SIGNALS and yields `required`; the corrupt-log read path cannot silently disarm any gate because record_ raises on corrupt JSON and close-out's check_activity_log_entry refuses to close an unreadable log, and the behavior mirrors the cross-provider-verified verificationMode readers). Round 2 VERIFIED. No release this session (PyPI ai_router release is Session 3).

reason.specifics: Routed recommendation (gemini-pro analysis, cross-provider to reduce self-bias): Session 2 wires the net-new content-aware close-out gate into ai_router/close_session.py and must MIRROR the dedicated_verification gate's shape -- both files the Session-1 author already mapped, so continuity of that architectural context outweighs fresh-eyes value.
